title: FPN Variants
concept: PANet and BiFPN extend FPN by adding bottom-up spatial pathways and weighted bidirectional feature connections.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why does FPN share weights across all prediction heads?

options.text: To keep parameter size small and prevent overfitting across different scales

exercises.explain: Correct! Sharing weights keeps the model size manageable while enabling scale-invariant heads.

cards.title: Beyond FPN
cards.body: FPN was a huge hit in 2017, but it had a limitation: information only flows down! What if we want low-level spatial details to flow back up the network? Let's explore modern variants like PANet and BiFPN.

cards.title: PANet & BiFPN Variants
cards.body: PANet adds a second, bottom-up pathway after FPN to transfer low-level spatial info back up. BiFPN (used in EfficientDet) introduces bidirectional connections and weighted feature fusion to allow features to flow dynamically up and down multiple times.

cards.title: The Two-Way Highway
cards.body: FPN is like a one-way street going down. PANet is like a two-way highway: cars can drive down, but a separate ramp allows them to drive back up. BiFPN is a complex multi-lane highway system with overpasses that let traffic cross anywhere.

cards.title: EfficientDet BiFPN
cards.body: EfficientDet uses BiFPN layers. Instead of just adding features, BiFPN assigns learning weights (coefficients) to each layer's input, realizing that some resolutions are more important than others for specific scales.

cards.title: Complex is Always Better?
cards.body: You might think BiFPN is always the best choice. Fact: Bidirectional layers add significant latency and memory overhead. For real-time applications on edge hardware, a simple FPN or PANet is often preferred for speed.

cards.title: Pyramid Variants Compared
cards.body: How pyramid architectures compare in information flow:

left.text:
FPN: Top-down only
PANet: Adds bottom-up path
PANet preserves corner features better

right.text:
Bidirectional flow loops
Weighted feature fusion
High accuracy, heavier compute

exercises.prompt: What pathway does PANet add to the standard FPN architecture?

options.text: A second, bottom-up path to carry low-level spatial details back up the pyramid
A recurrent feedback layer to crop the input
A classification head in the middle of the backbone
A dropout connection to reduce batch size

exercises.explain: Correct! PANet adds a bottom-up pathway to enrich high-level features with precise spatial details.

exercises.prompt: True or False: BiFPN uses weighted feature fusion, which means the network learns weights to decide how much each input layer contributes to the merged result.
exercises.explain: True. Weighted fusion allows BiFPN to balance features of different resolutions dynamically.

exercises.sentence: BiFPN introduces bidirectional flow, and is the core feature pyramid component in the ___ model family.
exercises.explain: BiFPN is the signature feature of the EfficientDet model family.

exercises.prompt: Which feature pyramid variant is generally preferred for ultra-fast, real-time edge devices?

options.text: A simple FPN or PANet, due to lower memory and computation overhead
A heavy 7-layer BiFPN, due to its maximum complexity
No pyramid at all, to keep the backbone linear

exercises.explain: Correct! Simple pyramids have much lower latency, which is crucial for real-time edge applications.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a high-precision medical imaging app to find micro-cracks in bones. You have plenty of GPU power and speed is not a concern. Which feature pyramid setup is best?

options.text: A model with BiFPN, because its weighted bidirectional feature fusion will maximize detection accuracy for micro-scale details.
A standard FPN with all upsampling disabled.
A single-scale YOLOv1 backbone with no pyramid.
A classification network with no bounding box outputs.

exercises.explain: Exactly! When accuracy is the sole priority, advanced pyramids like BiFPN provide superior multi-scale feature representations. Phase 8: Modern Object Detection is complete. Next up: Image Segmentation.
